summary: Warm, slightly tinny audience recording captures an outstanding 1981 show highlighted by Bird Song, Playing in the Band and a stellar first set.
review: The performance is frequently praised as one of the best of ’81 – a brilliant first set with Sugaree, Lazy Lightning and New Minglewood Blues, followed by a soaring second‑set sequence Bird > Samson > To Lay Me Down and a marathon Playing in the Band.  The audience source is clear enough to hear the band’s energy but sounds a bit remote, high‑end and lacking some Phil bass presence, which keeps the recording at a good rather than excellent level.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently described as exceptional (many 5‑star comments) while recording quality is generally good with noted high‑end/tinny issues; combined weighted score yields ~4.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Warm ambience but a bit too much high end and slightly tinny; clear enough to enjoy the performance but lacks full bass presence (Phil) and some ambience compared to soundboard mixes.

standout_songs: It Must Have Been The Roses
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is highlighted as a fan favorite with strong song placement; second set features the classic Bird > Samson > To Lay Me Down sequence and an epic Playing in the Band that showcases the band’s cohesion.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solos on New Minglewood Blues and overall guitar work are praised as spectacular.
band_member_comments.Phil: Several reviewers note a thin bass presence ('a little too Phil‑Lite') and that Phil is less audible in this audience mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s Hammond organ on New Minglewood Blues receives high praise; keyboards are described as fantastic.
